Next he gives the measures for the houses of mixed castes :
nun
The houses of persons of mixed classes such as Parasavas, sons
of BrShmanas by Sudra women, Bhurjakanfakas, sons of Brahmanas
by Vaisya women, and Murdhavasiktas, by Ksatriya women, should
have a measure equal to a half of the sum of the measures fixed for
the two classes of parents. It is inauspicious for alt people to have
houses which either exceed or fall short of the fixed measurements.
[The dimensions of a Parasava's house is equal to 32-}-20^-2 =
26 cubits; of a Bhurjakanfaka's 32-[-24-^-2--28 cubits; of a Murdha-
vasikta's 32+28^-2=30 cubits.
I have taken Utpala's explanation of Bhurjakanfaka, but
